chief_complaint: A 14-year-old boy presented with recurrent acute pancreatitis.
history: Double gallbladder is a rare congenital anomaly of the biliary system. The patient had recurrent acute pancreatitis. Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography revealed an accessory gallbladder draining into the main pancreatic duct, along with a filling defect suggestive of a stone within the accessory cystic duct. These findings did not suggest pancreaticobiliary maljunction.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Elevated amylase levels in the gallbladder bile and elevated bilirubin levels in the accessory gallbladder fluid, suggesting bidirectional reflux of pancreatic juice and bile.
correct_diagnosis: Double gallbladder with an accessory cystic duct draining into the main pancreatic duct.
differentials: Double gallbladder with an accessory cystic duct draining into the main pancreatic duct.
correct_ranking: Double gallbladder with an accessory cystic duct draining into the main pancreatic duct.
